mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 1-month basis, discount rates ease faster than growth expectations adjust, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during FEDFUNDS down-months, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks labor-market deterioration that anticipates weaker consumer income and risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad demand acceleration in real activity and retail spending, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive turns in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of joint-expansion instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO together with stable or falling CPIAUCSL signals disinflationary real output growth, which lowers recession risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO up and CPIAUCSL flat/down episodes are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the conditional spread versus baseline is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above recent history, the market expects tighter policy and a higher discount rate, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the short-horizon rank correlation is near zero for 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals weakening housing demand and tighter financing conditions, which usually leads broader cyclicals lower and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 2-month HOUST declines are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the conditional average return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, falling UNRATE together with rising PAYEMS signals labor reabsorption and stronger household income, which improves earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous UNRATE declines and PAYEMS gains do not yield positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth and GDPC1 growth both decelerate in a late-cycle expansion, the market should price weaker revenue growth and lower margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deceleration in RSAFS and GDPC1 is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the spread versus unconditional returns is not negative.
